channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: IEBC Requests Additional Sh887.9 Million to Support By-Elections and Operational Needs
item.description: The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) has formally requested a supplementary budget of Sh887.9 million for the 2025/2026 financial year to cover both ongoing operations and upcomi...

item.title: Police Investigate Suspected Arson of Vehicle on Carignan Street in Longueuil
item.description: Longueuil police are currently investigating a suspected case of vehicle arson on Carignan Street. Authorities responded to the scene after reports of a vehicle set on fire, though details regarding t...

item.title: Saskatoon City Takes Legal Action After Two Overpass Collisions in One Week
item.description: The City of Saskatoon is taking all legal steps following a second overpass collision within a week. On Wednesday, a semi-truck carrying an excavator struck the 108th Street overpass on Circle Drive i...

item.title: Job-seekers’ protest causes delays in waste collection across Joburg
item.description: Residents in several areas of Johannesburg, including Diepsloot, Cosmo City, and Zandspruit, are struggling with heaps of uncollected rubbish following a protest by frustrated job-seekers. The protest...

item.title: Main News Stories in Nigeria for Friday, 13th March 2026
item.description: Good morning Naija! Today na 13th March 2026, and plenty things dey happen for obodo. President Bola Tinubu don meet top Service Chiefs for Aso Rock to discuss di growing security wahala, especially f...

item.title: UK Food Regulator Tests Cultivated Meat Products for Safety Before Public Release
item.description: Lab-grown foie gras and chicken are undergoing safety evaluations in the UK, potentially bringing these 'cultivated meat' products to restaurants and households within the next five years, according t...

item.title: Sara Duterte expresses concern over bid for House custody of Madriaga
item.description: Former Vice President and Davao City Mayor Sara Duterte has expressed strong opposition to what she called a 'disturbing' attempt to secure custody of Ramil Madriaga by the House of Representatives. D...

item.title: Suspect killed after ramming car into Michigan synagogue
item.description: A man drove a vehicle into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield, Michigan, on Thursday, prompting a rapid response from law enforcement. The suspect, later identified by the Department of Homeland Securit...

item.title: Suspended physician David Edward-Ooi Poon faces 28 additional sex crime charges, bringing total to 71
item.description: Suspended physician David Edward-Ooi Poon, previously known for advocating family reunification during COVID-19 border restrictions, now faces a total of 71 criminal charges related to alleged sex cri...

item.title: Pakistan Faces Tough Choices Over Fuel Prices Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: As the US-Israel war with Iran disrupts Middle East oil markets, Pakistan is struggling to balance consumer protection with economic obligations to the IMF. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has blo...

item.title: Massive Fire Leaves 80 Shanties in Delhi Destroyed, Hundreds Displaced
item.description: A large-scale fire has reportedly razed around 80 makeshift homes in Delhi, leaving hundreds of residents homeless. While official reports are still awaited, preliminary accounts suggest that the blaz...

item.title: DR Congo Leaves Out Three Players Cited in Nigeria’s World Cup Eligibility Complaint
item.description: Fresh tori don dey surface for wahala wey dey between Nigeria and DR Congo over player eligibility for World Cup playoffs. Three players wey Nigeria Football Federation (NFF) mention for dem December ...

item.title: IEBC requests Sh59.38 million to conduct Emurua Dikirr by-election after MP's death
item.description: The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) in Kenya has formally requested Sh59.38 million from Parliament to run the Emurua Dikirr by-election following the death of MP Johanna Ngeno ...

item.title: Matildas face North Korea in Perth quarter-final with World Cup spot on the line
item.description: The Matildas' journey in the AFC Women's Asian Cup 2026 reaches a critical stage as they take on North Korea in a quarter-final clash at Perth Rectangular Stadium. The match carries added weight, as t...

item.title: Assessing the Impact of Mkhwanazi’s Allegations on Corruption and Accountability in South Africa
item.description: The July 2025 allegations by KwaZulu-Natal Police Commissioner Lieutenant-General Nhlanhla Mkhwanazi have stirred significant debate across South Africa’s political and law enforcement arenas. Mkhwana...

item.title: Top Accommodation Options for a Memorable Stay in Brisbane
item.description: Brisbane has transformed from a quiet country town into a vibrant, sophisticated city, offering a range of accommodation to suit all tastes. The Star Grand Brisbane, part of the Queen Wharf Brisbane P...

item.title: Exploring the Charm of Vintage Station Wagons from the 1960s
item.description: The article takes readers on a nostalgic journey through the world of vintage station wagons, highlighting their unique charm, practicality, and occasionally quirky designs. Once a staple of American ...

item.title: Qantas to pay $105 million in compensation for COVID-19 flight credit disputes
item.description: Qantas has reached a $105 million settlement in a class action lawsuit over flight credits issued instead of refunds for flights cancelled due to COVID-19. The settlement, which still requires Federal...

item.title: PMDC approves fee hike for selected medical and dental colleges, some applications deferred
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Pakistan Medical and Dental Council (PMDC) has recently approved 19 medical and dental colleges to increase their annual fees up to Rs2,147,483. This move comes after years of fee cappi...

item.title: Sandiganbayan denies Jinggoy Estrada's request for Ombudsman investigation records
item.description: The Sandiganbayan has officially denied Senator Jinggoy Estrada’s request for the Ombudsman to submit its preliminary investigation records in connection with his ongoing graft trial involving 11 coun...
